title: On A Magazine Sonnet
content:
"Scorn not the sonnet," though its strength be sapped,
        Nor say malignant its inventor blundered;
    The corpse that here in fourteen lines is wrapped
        Had otherwise been covered with a hundred.
meaning:
Russell Hilliard Loines's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "On A Magazine Sonnet"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
